Lighter-colored communes with low charger counts appear along the coast: Calvi (northwest), Corte (north-central), Aléria and Ghisonaccia (east coast), Zonza, Sartène, Porto-Vecchio, and Bonifacio (south). All inland and mountainous communes are in light gray, indicating 0 chargers. A map annotation labels the mountainous interior as 'Region montagneuse'. No commune is selected, and no tooltip is visible.

Tooltips are open for the Centre box showing from top to bottom: outlier L'Isle-d'Abeau '(Centre, max: 59,52 %)', upper fence '(Centre, upper fence: 56,35 %)', q3 '(Centre, q3: 44,86 %)', median '(Centre, median: 41,105 %)', q1 '(Centre, q1: 37,095 %)', lower fence '(Centre, lower fence: 28,64 %)', and outlier Die '(Centre, min: 22,43 %)'.

prompt: Generate an aria-label for this data visualization image.
aria_label: This dashboard titled 'Tourisme - Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes' shows tourism data for the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. The Région dropdown is set to 'Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes', and the 'Tourisme' view tab is selected at top right (the alternate 'Démographie et Transports' tab is unselected). Five key performance indicators in the left column show: 14.6M arrivals with a net increase 2,3% in 2024 versus 2023, 8.81 nuitées par habitant in 2024, 604.83 arrivées par km² in 2024, and 2.595K itinéraires touristiques. A seasonal table titled 'Saisonnalité touristique - Nuitées par mois et année' lists overnight stays by month with columns for 2022, 2023, and 2024; months January through November are visible (December is cut off). August is the visible peak across all three years, with printed values 2,793,000 (2022) and 2,626,000 (2023). A combo chart titled 'Dépense touristique annuelle et Part dans le PIB par région' shows tourist spending as bars (left Y-axis 'Dépense touristique (en M€)' with ticks at 0, 20K, 40K) overlaid with each region's share of GDP as an orange line (right Y-axis 'Part dans le PIB' with ticks at 0 %, 5 %, 10 %, 15 %). The X-axis 'Région' lists 12 French regions left to right, sorted by descending bar height: Île-de-France, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Occitanie, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Bretagne, Grand Est, Pays de la Loire, Hauts-de-France, Normandie, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, and Centre-Val de Loire. A map titled 'Itinéraires touristiques' shows the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France with point markers for tourist itineraries. A gauge titled 'Score touristique' shows 0.65 on a scale from 0.00 to 1.00.
